local_kernel.label: Local Kernel Runtime
local_kernel.description: Built-in local runtime used by the Meridian quickstart and governed workspace demo. Agents run in the same process or via subprocess on the local machine.
local_kernel.notes: Reference implementation. All seven contract requirements satisfied natively. Used by kernel/workspace.py and examples/intelligence/.

loom_native.label: Meridian Loom Runtime
loom_native.description: Meridian-native execution runtime in Rust. Primary runtime for the Meridian stack with session management, capability dispatch, and governed worker execution.
loom_native.notes: Primary Meridian runtime. Loom provides native 7/7 contract compliance through governed capability dispatch, session provenance, and PoGE receipts.

openfang_compatible.label: OpenFang-Compatible Runtime
openfang_compatible.description: Open-source agentic runtime placeholder. Meridian can govern agents from OpenFang-compatible runtimes once an adapter satisfying the constitutional contract is implemented.
openfang_compatible.notes: Placeholder. No adapter implemented yet. Contract compliance unknown pending runtime API review.

mcp_generic.label: Generic MCP Runtime
mcp_generic.description: Any agent runtime that exposes tools and context via the Model Context Protocol (MCP, EIP 2024). Agent identity maps to MCP client ID. Actions map to tool calls.
mcp_generic.notes: MCP provides agent identity via client ID and action envelope via tool call schema. Cost attribution, approval hooks, audit emission, sanction controls, and budget gate all require a Meridian MCP middleware adapter. Planned for v0.2.

a2a_generic.label: Generic A2A Runtime
a2a_generic.description: Any agent-to-agent capable runtime implementing the A2A interoperability protocol. Agent identity maps to A2A agent card. Inter-agent calls map to the action envelope.
a2a_generic.notes: A2A provides stable agent identity via agent card. Action envelope mapping from A2A task schema is partial. All treasury, audit, court integration requires a Meridian A2A adapter. Planned for v0.2.

legacy_v1_compatible.label: Legacy V1 Compatibility Bridge
legacy_v1_compatible.description: Kernel-side compatibility bridge for pre-Loom integration paths. Used only for bounded legacy federation and adapter proof paths.

adapter_proof.notes: Kernel-side bridge that keeps bounded legacy integration paths governable while Loom remains the primary Meridian runtime.

legacy_v1_compatible.notes: Compatibility-only runtime seam. Not the primary Meridian runtime.

contract_requirements.agent_identity: Runtime must provide a stable, unique agent ID that maps to a Meridian agent record. Identity must persist across sessions.
contract_requirements.action_envelope: Runtime must wrap each governed action with: agent_id, action_type, resource, estimated_cost_usd. Envelope is the input to check_authority() and check_budget().
contract_requirements.cost_attribution: Runtime must report actual cost post-action via spend reporting hook so Meridian can update metering records.
contract_requirements.approval_hook: Runtime must call check_authority(agent_id, action) before executing privileged actions and must block if the result is False.
contract_requirements.audit_emission: Runtime must emit structured audit events that Meridian can ingest into audit_log.jsonl. Minimum fields: timestamp, agent_id, action, outcome.
contract_requirements.sanction_controls: Runtime must query get_restrictions(agent_id) before each agent session and must disable or restrict agents under active sanctions.
contract_requirements.budget_gate: Runtime must call check_budget(agent_id, cost) before cost-bearing operations and must block if the result is False.
